title: 6 Managing and Deleting Users
body_text:
6	Managing and Deleting Users
6.1	Overview of Managing Users
The procedures in this chapter apply to both Client Centre Users and User Management Security Officers (Security Officers) unless otherwise noted.
Security Officers are responsible for managing Client Centre Users. For the most part, Security Officers can modify and delete other Security Officers via the User Management application. Your financial institution must appoint at least two Security Officers to manage users; however, when determining the number of security officers you require, ensure that you consider coverage for absences. Central 1 can芒聙聶t manage Client Centre Users on your behalf.
Security Officers manage users as follows:
Create Client Centre Users.
Note:	Security Officers can芒聙聶t create other Security Officers. See Section 4.2, Adding a User Management Security Officer, for procedures.
Manage application access and, if applicable, modify permissions associated with an application. See Chapter 8, Managing Application Access and Permissions, for procedures.
Manage administrative access to applications for other Security Officers provided they also have administrative access to the application See Section 4.4, Assigning Administrative Rights to a User Management Security Officer, for procedures.
Add, remove, or unlock a 2-step security token. See Chapter 7, Managing 2-Step Security Tokens.
Update contact information (e.g., email, phone number).
Update profile status (e.g., from 芒聙聹Inactive芒聙聺 to 芒聙聹Active芒聙聺).
Reset Client Centre User芒聙聶s passwords.
Note:	Security Officers can芒聙聶t reset passwords for other Security Officers. Those requests must be made in writing to Central 1, as described in Section 4.3, Modifying or Deleting a User Management Security Officer. Security Officers can reset their own passwords in User Management. Another Security Officer must approve the change.
Delete a profile.
The procedures for managing business users are the same as those for Client Centre Users, unless otherwise noted.
6.2	Creating a Client Centre User
Create a Client Centre user as follows:
Log in to User Management as described in Chapter 5, Accessing and Navigating User Management.
Select
Manage User
, then select
Create User
. See Figure 1.
Note:	Security Officers can芒聙聶t create other Security Officers. See Section 4.2, Adding a User Management Security Officer, for procedures.
Figure 1: Select Create User Tab
Choose
Select Organization
. See Figure 2.
Note:	Your financial institution芒聙聶s name displays in the Organization field.
Figure 2: Select Organization
Complete the User Information page. Completion of the page is self-explanatory, except as noted. See Figure 3.
芒聭
Username
芒聙聯 The username must be a minimum of 3 characters, can芒聙聶t include spaces, and can芒聙聶t exceed 20 characters. The username can only consist of alphanumeric characters, hyphens 芒聙聹-芒聙聺, and underscores 芒聙聹_芒聙聺. Hyphens and underscores can芒聙聶t occur consecutively (for example, 芒聙聹芒聙聰芒聙聺 or 芒聙聹 __芒聙聺). Optionally, use the 芒聙聹Check if username is available芒聙聺 feature to confirm the username isn芒聙聶t already in use.
芒聭隆
One-time password
芒聙聯 Select
Generate new one-time password
to generate a unique password for the new user. The one-time password must be:
芒聢聮
communicated in a secure and confidential manner, and
芒聢聮
changed by the user as soon as possible.
The first time the user logs in to the Client Centre, they芒聙聶re prompted to enter their one-time password and to create a new one. One-time passwords are a pass phrase (four words or a set of numbers with spaces between them).
Important:
One-time passwords must be entered exactly as generated, including the spaces.
The new password must be a minimum of eight characters and can contain alphanumeric and special characters, including capital letters. It can芒聙聶t be the same as the last six passwords.
Passwords are checked against a list of unsecure passwords known to have been exposed or shared from internet data breaches. This means that some password changes will fail even though the new password meets the structure requirements. If this occurs, the user must select a new password or use a passphrase instead, as these are harder to guess (e.g., 芒聙聹deskchairphonemug芒聙聺 or 芒聙聹I LOVE ice Cream day芒聙聺).
芒聭垄
User Type
芒聙聯
Select the appropriate user type from the drop-down menu.
Note:	The Business User type applies to businesses with access to
PaymentStream
AFT.
Figure 3: Creating a User
If you芒聙聶re creating a Business User:
Begin typing the business name to display matches. Select the appropriate business when it displays in the Business field. See Figure聽4.
Note: 	Applies to businesses set up on
PaymentStream
AFT.
Figure 4: Choosing the Business
Select
Submit
. See Figure 4.
Review the user information on the confirmation page.
Select
Confirm
to create the user. Alternatively, select
Edit
or
Cancel
as required.  See Figure聽5.
Figure 5: Select Confirm to Create User
Select
Continue and Select Applications
to grant the user access to applications. See Figure 6. You can only assign applications to which you have administrative rights. See Chapter 8, Managing Application Access and Permissions, for information on assigning specific applications to the user.
Figure 6: Select 芒聙聹Continue and Select Applications芒聙聺
Note:	If the user is new and doesn芒聙聶t access the Client Centre or any application via the Client Centre within 15 days following the account creation date, Central 1 will update the user芒聙聶s account status to inactive.
6.3	Searching for a User
Search for Client Centre Users and Security Officers as follows:
Log in to User Management as described in Section 5.1, Accessing User Management.
Select
Manage User
, then select
Modify User
. See Figure 7.
Figure 7: Select Modify User
Complete the appropriate search fields, then select
Search
. See Figure 8.
芒聢聮
Complete the Business field to search for business users.
Note:	Business users won芒聙聶t display unless the business field is completed.
芒聢聮
Complete the Name field to search for a user by first name, last name, or username.
芒聢聮
Select an Application Name from the drop-down menu to search for a list of users that have access to that application.
Figure 8: Completing the User Search Fields
Select the appropriate user from the list of search results. See Figure 9. The User Type column displays the type of user (e.g., Credit Union User, Security Officer, or Business User).
Figure 9: User Search Results
Modify the user as required. See the appropriate section in this chapter for information on modifying specific user parameters. See Figure 10.
Figure 10: Modify User Page
6.4	Updating a User芒聙聶s Contact Information
Update a Client Centre User芒聙聶s or Security Officer芒聙聶s contact information as follows:
Search for the user as described in Section 6.3, Searching for a User.
Select
Edit User Information
. See Figure 11.
Figure 11: Edit User Information
Modify the user芒聙聶s name and contact information as required, then select
Submit
. See Figure聽12.
Note:	You can芒聙聶t change the profile芒聙聶s user type.
Figure 12: Modify User
Review the changes on the confirmation page, then select
Confirm.
Alternatively, select
Edit
or
Cancel
as required.
Have another Security Officer approve the request, as described in Chapter 9, Managing Requests.
6.5	Changing a User芒聙聶s Account Status
Central 1 disables inactive accounts after 13 months of inactivity. An account is deemed inactive if a Client Centre User or Security Officer hasn芒聙聶t logged in to the Client Centre for 13 months. Security Officers can manually change an account芒聙聶s status to inactive or enable an inactive account.
Change the account status of a Client Centre User芒聙聶s or Security Officer芒聙聶s profile as follows:
Search for the user as described in Section 6.3, Searching for a User.
Select
Change Account Status
. See Figure 13.
Figure 13: Select Change Account Status
Select
Active
or
Inactive
(as appropriate) from the Status drop-down menu, then select
Submit
. See Figure 14.
Figure 14: Change Account Status
Review the change on the confirmation page, then select
Confirm
. Alternatively, select
Edit
or
Cancel
as required.
Have another Security Officer approve the change as described in Chapter 9, Managing Requests.
6.6	Resetting a User芒聙聶s Password
Use the reset password function to reset a Client Centre user芒聙聶s password and generate a new one-time password. Security Officers can also reset their own password in User Management; however, another Security Officer must approve the change.
Note:	Security Officers can芒聙聶t reset another Officer芒聙聶s password. These requests must be sent to Central 1. See Section 4.3, Modifying or Deleting a User Management Security Officer, for procedures.
Reset a user芒聙聶s password as follows:
Search for the user as described in Section 6.3, Searching for a User.
Select
Reset Password
. See Figure 15.
Figure 15: Select Reset Password
Select
Generate new one-time password
, then select
Submit
. See Figure 16. A new one-time password displays.
Figure 16: Generate a One-time Password
Review the change on the confirmation page, then select
Confirm
. Alternatively, select
Edit
or
Cancel
as required.
If you芒聙聶re resetting your own password, have another Security Officer approve the change as described in Chapter 9, Managing Requests.
Important
:	Communicate the one-time password in a secure and confidential manner. Users must change their password as quickly as possible. One-time passwords are a pass phrase (four words or a set of numbers with spaces between them).
Important
:	One-time passwords must be entered exactly as generated, including the spaces.
Users are prompted to enter the one-time password and create a new password the next time they log in to the Client Centre.
6.7	Deleting a User
Delete users that don芒聙聶t require Client Centre or User Management access, or if they芒聙聶re no longer your employee. Deleting a Client Centre User ID is permanent. The deleted user ID can芒聙聶t be used again.
Delete a user as follows:
Search for the user as described in Section 6.3, Searching for a User.
Select
Close User Account
. See Figure 17.
Figure 17: Select Close User Account
Select
OK
to confirm the deletion. Deleting a user doesn芒聙聶t require approval from another Security Officer.
Note:	Deleting a Client Centre User ID is permanent. The deleted user ID can芒聙聶t be used again.
6.8	Inactive Accounts
An account is deemed inactive if a Client Centre User or Security Officer hasn芒聙聶t logged in to the Client Centre for 13 months, or if the user is new and doesn芒聙聶t access the Client Centre or any application via the Client Centre within 15 days of the account creation.
For added security, Central聽1 disables inactive accounts after 13 months of inactivity. Disabled accounts can be activated within 90 days, if required. See Section 6.5, Changing a User芒聙聶s Account Status, for procedures. After 90 days, a disabled account is deleted. An account deleted by Central 1 can be recovered for up to 90 days. Contact Client Support to recover a deleted inactive account. See 1.2, Contacts, Websites, and Other Resources.
6.9	Managing
MemberDirect
脗庐
Forms Access
MemberDirect
Forms Access allows authorized users at your financial institution to retrieve customer form data completed on
MemberDirect
or Forge and sent by secure email. Users access the data via an embedded link in the email using their Client Centre username and password. See
MemberDirect
Integrated Services Standard Forms Tour Guide for more information.
Set up
MemberDirect
Forms Access as follows:
Open the user芒聙聶s profile as described in Section 6.3, Searching for a User.
Select
Edit
next to 芒聙聹MemberDirect Forms Access芒聙聺. See Figure 18.
Figure 18: Edit MemberDirect Forms Access
Select the check box(es) next to the appropriate
MemberDirect
Forms Access permission(s) (MDI, MDB, or both), then select
Submit
. See Figure 19.
Figure 19: Complete MemberDirect Forms Access Permissions
Review the summary of changes, then select
Confirm
. Alternatively, select
Edit
or
Cancel
.
Have another Security Officer approve the change as described in Chapter 9, Managing Requests.
Related Information
MemberDirect Integrated Standard Forms Tour Guide
END
